mission: Determine whether the ordinary holomorphic divisor route from puncture data on the compactified horizon sphere can produce the residual spin^c twist after canonical spin normalization.

theorem_1_gauge_compatible_divisor_degre.statement: Consider any ordinary divisor transfer on the compactified horizon sphere of the form D = Σ_i m_i p_i with associated line bundle O(D). If the transfer is compatible with the standard physical ABCK gauge quotient, diagonal U(1) acts trivially and the total weight must vanish. Therefore the transferred divisor has degree zero: deg D = Σ_i m_i = 0.

theorem_2_cp1_pic0_trivial.statement: On the Riemann sphere S^2 ≅ CP^1, every holomorphic line bundle is O(n) for a unique integer n = deg L, and Pic^0(CP^1) = 0. Therefore every degree-zero divisor line bundle is holomorphically trivial.
external_support: standard algebraic geometry of CP^1 / Riemann sphere line bundles

theorem_3_standard_divisor_route_dead.statement: Combining gauge-compatible degree zero with Pic^0(CP^1)=0, the ordinary divisor construction from puncture data cannot generate the missing residual determinant twist on the current stack. Any standard holomorphic puncture-divisor transfer compatible with the physical ABCK gauge quotient produces only the trivial extra line bundle.

imports.charged_boundary: After canonical spin normalization, every remaining charged current-stack route is exhausted. There is no physical non-neutral surface sector, no derived transfer from puncture-moduli line data to an extra determinant line on S^2, and no hidden CP-odd inflow inside the standard surface Chern-Simons sector. The only positive future route is genuinely new charged boundary physics beyond the present stack.

still_not_derived: a non-neutral charged surface sector beyond the standard ABCK quotient
a non-holomorphic or non-divisor transfer from puncture data to S^2 determinant line data
an independent CP-odd seed or anomaly-inflow theorem

final_boundary.status: derived / exhausted ordinary divisor-transfer Paper 35 boundary
final_boundary.safe_statement: The ordinary puncture-divisor route is dead. On the compactified sphere, any gauge-compatible divisor transfer has degree zero, and on CP^1 every degree-zero line bundle is trivial. So standard holomorphic puncture divisors cannot supply the residual spin^c twist on the current stack.
